name: Greg Dulcich
headline: Healthy at last, Dulcich could finally own Miami's TE room
body:
Dulcich's 2025 efficiency numbers are the reason to care. Across nine games he turned 33 targets into 26 grabs (78.8% catch rate, zero drops) for 335 yards and a 12.88 average, posting 2.36 yards per route and a sterling 0.559 EPA per play with a 60.6% success rate. That's a wide leap from his disastrous 2024 split between Denver and New York, where he managed a minus-0.634 EPA and an 8.3% success rate on 12 targets. The talent flashes; staying on the field has been the obstacle.

The opportunity now lines up. He sits atop the depth chart for an offense thin on proven receiving options, and a tight-end-friendly staff plus a strong spring with Malik Willis point toward real volume. His 7.1% target share and 0.232 targets per route still leave room to climb, and a projection near 53 catches and 600 yards would be a career high. Durability is the only thing standing between him and a streaming-plus role.
